description: Fix 2 documentation inaccuracies found during a systematic audit of all 24 docs pages against the source code: a missing API parameter in the SDK utilities reference, and missing audit log persistence information in the MCP server reference.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add missing signal option to waitUntil() options table in SDK utilities reference
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the waitUntil() documentation to show the signal option so that I know I can cancel polling early with an AbortSignal.
acceptanceCriteria: The waitUntil() options table in utilities.mdx includes a row for `signal` with type `AbortSignal`, default `—`, and description explaining it cancels polling early
The waitUntil() section includes a code example demonstrating AbortSignal usage (matching the pattern already shown in the retry() section above it)
The `lastUpdated` frontmatter in utilities.mdx is set to `2026-02-28`
userStories.notes:
File to edit: docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx

The waitUntil() options table is around lines 678-701. It currently has 2 rows: `interval` (number, default 200) and `timeout` (number, default 10000). The source code at platform/plugin-sdk/src/timing.ts line 18-25 shows WaitUntilOptions also has `signal?: AbortSignal`.

The retry() options table on the same page (around lines 594-635) already correctly documents the `signal` option — use it as the pattern. The retry signal row (lines 629-633) reads:
- Option: `signal`
- Type: `AbortSignal`
- Default: `—`
- Description: Cancel retries early. Checks before each attempt and before each delay.

For waitUntil, the description should be something like: 'Cancel polling early. The signal is checked between polling intervals.'

Also add a usage example after the existing examples (around line 712), similar to the retry cancellation example. Something like:
```typescript
// Cancel polling after 5 seconds
const controller = new AbortController();
setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), 5000);
await waitUntil(
  () => getTextContent('.status') === 'Ready',
  { signal: controller.signal },
);
```

The frontmatter lastUpdated field is on line 4 — change the date to 2026-02-28.

userStories.title: Add persistent audit log documentation to MCP server reference
userStories.description: As a user, I want the MCP server reference to mention that audit entries are persisted to disk so that I know the full invocation history survives server restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: The /audit section in mcp-server.mdx mentions that entries are also persisted to `~/.opentabs/audit.log` as NDJSON
The note mentions file rotation at 10 MB (keeping audit.log + audit.log.1)
The note cross-references `opentabs audit --file` for querying the full disk-based log
The added text is concise (1-2 sentences, not a new section)
userStories.notes:
File to edit: docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx

The /audit section starts around line 234. Line 236 says: 'Returns tool invocations as a JSON array, newest first. Requires Bearer token authentication. The server maintains a circular buffer of the last 500 entries.'

Add a sentence after that paragraph (before the curl example) mentioning disk persistence. Something like: 'Entries are also persisted to `~/.opentabs/audit.log` as NDJSON with automatic rotation at 10 MB. Use `opentabs audit --file` to query the full disk-based log, which survives server restarts.'

Source reference: platform/mcp-server/src/audit-disk.ts (MAX_AUDIT_FILE_SIZE = 10 * 1024 * 1024, rotation keeps audit.log + audit.log.1).

The page lastUpdated is already 2026-02-28, so no date change needed.
